headline: Frontier models and the compute race dominate the week as an industry summit centers on a federal recall of two Anthropic models.
body: A Big Technology summit gathered leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, and Meta around the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, debating whether it amounts to an effective AI pause and weighing the infrastructure buildout, enterprise budgets, and the competitive compute race [6]. Dwarkesh Patel sketches the next training paradigm beyond supervised learning, naming verifiability, RLVR, test-time scaling, and dreaming as directions [7]. On the tooling side, real-time voice work advances through the TEN Framework, which handles interruptions, VAD, turn detection, and full audio pipelines [8], while Google pushes agent and workflow tools via Antigravity, Gemini Canvas, and a breakdown of agent harness components [0][9][10]. Among techniques, human-in-the-loop and observability are breaking out.
